title: Shoko raMwari
subtitle: The New Covenant 1841 — Book of Light: Pre-Shona Mathematics Entry Gate
author: ABASID 1841

verses.text: VERSE 1. *THE NEW COVENANT 1841* *BOOK OF LIGHT — THE PRE-SHONA MATHEMATICS ENTRY GATE* *By ABASID 1841 (RA
VERSE 2. MUSIYIWA, “the One Le� to Return”)*
VERSE 3. In the beginning we did not write; we listened.
VERSE 4. We listened to breath, to hea�beats, to footsteps.
VERSE 5. We noticed that things return in a pa�ern.
VERSE 6. A pa�ern is counting you can hear.
VERSE 7. Counting in *Shona is kuverenga* — it also means to remember.
VERSE 8. When we count, we remember how God moves.
VERSE 9. Sounds became our �rst numbers because each sound kept its strength the same way.
VERSE 10. The elders taught: “Every sound has a number, and every number has a job.”
VERSE 11. That is why our alphabet is also a math language.
VERSE 12. We call it the *Sacred Alphabet of Shona — 22 sounds, each with a value.* *I AM A SA RA — I AM 1 8 4 1 — NDINI NDEGA ASARA NENZIRA YA ASAR — IIIII  SA KA USAIRASE NZIRA MWANA*
VERSE 13. Read slowly. Each verse tells the name, the number, and the simple meaning. These are tools a child can use, and a sage can never �nish.

VERSE 14. *A = 1* — First breath. Sta� of everything. Before you speak, you breathe. When you breathe, you remember that life begins inside you �rst. Every “ *A* ” you say is you calling God’s �rst light. A MUNHU
VERSE 15. *BA = 2* — Mother’s house. Two means pair. Two arms hold the child. Two eyes see the world.
VERSE 16. *BA* ” means the warmth that keeps beginnings safe. BA BA
VERSE 17. *KA = 3* — Child. One and two together make new life. When *A* and *BA* meet, something new laughs between them. *KA* is that laugh—the small �ame that proves love is working. MUKA KA
VERSE 18. *RA = 4* — Sun. Light that helps us see the way. *RA* is the bright elder that never forgets to rise. He teaches that light is a duty, not a show. To shine is to serve. NDABA RA VANORWA RA
VERSE 19. *MA = 5* — Moon. So� light that teaches timing. *MA* is the quiet sister who counts time with her shape. She says, “Grow, rest, grow again.” Her lesson: change is not loss; it is rhythm.
VERSE 20. *DA = 6* — Love. The bridge that holds us together. *DA* is the bridge made by giving without asking back. It turns strangers into kin and pain into peace. NDINO DA ANO DA
VERSE 21. *NA = 7* — Connection. Threads between people and places. *NA* is the rope of stories between people. Every time you greet, you tie another knot of life. FAMBAI NA IYE
VERSE 22. *SA = 8* — Likeness. A pa�ern that repeats. SA whispers, “You are shaped like your Maker.” That is why we mirror kindness and not cruelty. IVAI SA IYE
VERSE 23. *ZI = 9* — Quiet completion. The pause before the next step. *ZI* is the pause a�er music. It is the quiet where the next idea hides. Only patient ears hear it. KUNYARARA KUTI ZI
VERSE 24. *TA = 10* — “We are.” A full hand. A new line begins. Ten �ngers, ten friends. *TA* says no one builds alone; together is the true number. TA NAMA TA TA DZIDZISA
VERSE 25. *VA = 20* — “They are.” Many hands working. *VA* is the crowd singing one song. It reminds you that strength grows when voices join. VA PENYU VA PONA
VERSE 26. *NGU = 30* — Time. The drum that keeps the pace. The drumbeat of seasons— *NGU* —never lies. It keeps the promise that morning always follows night.
VERSE 27. *NDA = 40* — “I am.” Standing �rm inside truth. *NDA* stands tall and says, “I know who I am.” It is courage dressed in truth. NDA TAURISA NDA ZORORA
VERSE 28. *PA = 50* — Giving. Opening the hand to share. *PA* opens the hand. It proves that owning is smaller than sharing. NDINO PA ANO PA
VERSE 29. *HA = 60* — Boundary. A wise “no” that keeps order. *HA* is a holy fence. It protects gardens, hea�s, and promises. HA NDIDI HA NDINA HA ENDE HA GARI HA ZVIITE
VERSE 30. *GA = 70* — Planting/Instilling. Pu�ing lessons in the soil of the hea�. *GA* puts seeds—ideas or maize—into good ground. It trusts the unseen future.
VERSE 31. *SHU = 80* — Wind/Spirit. What you cannot see but can feel. *SHU* moves what eyes can’t see. It teaches that life is bigger than the body.
VERSE 32. *MBA = 90* — House/Body. A safe place for life. *MBA* is where spirit rests. Keep it clean, feed it light, and it will guard your soul.
VERSE 33. *NYU = 100* — Water/Memory. Water remembers the path. *NYU* �ows and remembers. It teaches forgiveness, for rivers forget the stones that hu� them.
VERSE 34. *CHI = 200* — “What?” The doorway of questions. *CHI* asks questions so truth can walk in. Never fear asking; fear staying blind.
VERSE 35. *TO = 300* — Air/Judgment. Clear thinking, fair weighing. *TO* is breath thinking clearly. It weighs words before speaking them.

VERSE 36. *NGA = 400* — Crown/Heaven. The high place of purpose. *NGA* rises above, yet bows to all below. It is the end that circles back to beginning. *I AM A SA RA — I AM 1 8 4 1 — NDINI NDEGA ASARA NENZIRA YA ASAR — IIIII  SA KA USAIRASE NZIRA MWANA*
VERSE 39. Why? Because *1+2+3+4* makes a full set
VERSE 40. ten �ngers
VERSE 41. the symbol of “ *We are*
VERSE 42. *TA* KUFUNDA IZWI .”
VERSE 43. - *TA* MUMBIRI YA BABA JOHANI - SHONA - IZWI .”
VERSE 44. A�er *TA* (10), we step to *VA* (20) — bigger groups, bigger work.
VERSE 45. Numbers grow, but their jobs stay the same. That is the *secret of memory* . *I AM A SA RA — I AM 1 8 4 1 — NDINI NDEGA ASARA NENZIRA YA ASAR — IIIII  SA KA USAIRASE NZIRA MWANA*
VERSE 46. The elders taught a name that is also a number: *A SA RA A.* The elders said every age is sealed by a number that sings its story.
VERSE 47. When we write its values, we see *1 8 4 1.* In our age the song is *A SA RA A → 1 8 4 1.*
VERSE 48. Read it slowly: *A*   (1), *SA* (8), *RA* (4), *A*    (1). These four sounds are four steps of the same journey:
VERSE 49. 1]  *A* = beginning,
VERSE 50. 8]  *SA* = pa�ern,
VERSE 51. 4]  *RA* = light,
VERSE 52. 1]  *A* = return.
VERSE 53. This is not random. It is a path: *Sta� ( 1   A),* *Pa�ern ( 8   SA),* *Light ( 4   RA),* *Return ( 1  A).* Together they tell how life moves: sta�, grow, shine, come home.
VERSE 54. Add them: *1 + 8 + 4 + 1 = 14* Also
VERSE 55. *1 + 4 = 5* which is
VERSE 56. *MA*
VERSE 57. Wisdom/Moon). The seal is read in full: *A (1) + SA (8) + RA (4) + A (1) = 14 → where 1 + 4 = 5 and 5 = MA*
VERSE 58. Wisdom).
VERSE 59. Meaning: *The Seal of A SA RA A* returns with wisdom. This means the path of *A SA RA A* always ends in wisdom. To walk it is to learn gently.
VERSE 60. This is why we say *ABASID 1841* carries the lamp of returning wisdom.

VERSE 61. *One Eight Four One* ” is therefore not a secret code but a mirror. Whoever looks kindly into it sees light returning to its source. *I AM A SA RA — I AM 1 8 4 1 — NDINI NDEGA ASARA NENZIRA YA ASAR — IIIII  SA KA USAIRASE NZIRA MWANA*
VERSE 62. Long ago, people spoke of *ASAR / OSIRIS* — the One who dies and rises. The elders called this mirror *ABASID 1841* — the one who remembers both the sta� and the return.
VERSE 63. In our tongue we hear the echo: *A SA RA A.* For in every breath, the �rst *A* goes out and the last *A* comes back; that is resurrection in rhythm.
VERSE 64. The story is the same pa�ern: fall, rest, rise, return. Even a child can feel it: breathe out *(A)* → pause *(SA)*
VERSE 65. shine *(RA)*
VERSE 66. breathe in *(A)* . That is the whole world inside one breath.

VERSE 67. That is why *ABASID 1841* is called the *Master of Resurrection* — not by boast, but by pa�ern. So when you say *A SA RA A,* you are doing more than speaking — you are keeping the sun alive inside you.
VERSE 68. The bi�h name *MUSIYIWA* means “le�,” “le� behind,” or simply “ *le�* .”
VERSE 69. In *Shona, ASARA* also means “ *le�* ” or “ le� behind .”
VERSE 70. The two agree like mirror and face: the one le� so the one may return.
VERSE 71. Therefore, the name, the number, and the story match.
VERSE 72. And when names, numbers, and stories match, the elders say:
VERSE 73. This is not accident; this is calling.” *I AM A SA RA — I AM 1 8 4 1 — NDINI NDEGA ASARA NENZIRA YA ASAR — IIIII  SA KA USAIRASE NZIRA MWANA*
VERSE 74. Say the ten with your �ngers: *A(1),* BA(2), KA(3), *RA(4),* MA(5), DA(6), NA(7), *SA(8),* ZI(9), TA(10).
VERSE 75. Now whisper the seal: *A SA RA A → 1, 8, 4, 1.*
VERSE 77. Speak the meaning:
VERSE 78. The Seal returns with wisdom.”
VERSE 79. Remember the big truth we �xed today: *1+2+3+4 = TA* (10).
VERSE 80. If you forget, count your �ngers. Truth is friendly. *I AM A SA RA — I AM 1 8 4 1 — NDINI NDEGA ASARA NENZIRA YA ASAR — IIIII  SA KA USAIRASE NZIRA MWANA*
VERSE 81. May every child who reads these sounds feel safe with numbers.
VERSE 82. May every elder who teaches these numbers keep them simple and true.
VERSE 83. May the pa�ern of *A SA RA A (1841)* light your path back home.
VERSE 84. And when you feel “ *le�* ,” remember: being le� can be the doorway to return. *I AM A SA RA — I AM 1 8 4 1 — NDINI NDEGA ASARA NENZIRA YA ASAR — IIIII  SA KA USAIRASE NZIRA MWANA*
VERSE 85. Sit still. Take a slow breath. Let it come in like the wind and leave like the tide.
VERSE 86. Feel your hea� beat — that is the �rst drum of counting.
VERSE 87. Now say so�ly: *A …SA …RA …A* .
VERSE 88. Do not rush. Each sound has its own step. *A* — the beginning. *SA* — the pa�ern of life. *RA* — the light that guides. *A* — the return to peace.
VERSE 89. Say it again, slower: *A ...SA ...RA ...A.* Let the breath �nish its circle.
VERSE 90. You have just spoken the oldest equation: *1 + 8 + 4 + 1 = 14 → 1
VERSE 91. 4 = 5 = MA* .
VERSE 92. It means:
VERSE 93. Wisdom is born when the journey of light returns to its source.”
VERSE 94. Now, count with your hands. Li� one �nger for *A* , two for *BA* , three for *KA* , four for *RA* .
VERSE 95. Look at your hand. How many �ngers stand? Ten. That is *TA = 10*
VERSE 96. “We are.”
VERSE 97. You have found the secret inside your own hand: *1 + 2 + 3 + 4 = TA* . The full hand means full life.
VERSE 98. Every time you help another hand, the circle grows — that is *VA* (20), the work of many.
VERSE 99. Every day you wait for the sun to rise, you keep time — that is *NGU* (30), the song of the drum.
VERSE 100. When you speak truth and say “I am,” you stand in *NDA* (40), the pillar of being.
VERSE 101. When you give, you open *PA* (50), the gate of kindness.
VERSE 102. When you set healthy borders, you live in *HA* (60), the land of peace.
VERSE 103. And when you breathe out a�er reading these words, you will feel *SHU* (80)
VERSE 104. the wind that carries your spirit home.
VERSE 105. Do this o�en: Say *A SA RA A* with breath, with stillness, with kindness. It is not only words — it is remembering who you are.
VERSE 106. Say it when you wake. Say it when you are afraid. Say it when you feel forgo�en.
VERSE 107. For each time you speak it, you light again the lamp of *1841* , the lamp that never truly went out.
VERSE 108. Now whisper:
VERSE 109. I am not lost. I am on the path of return.”
VERSE 110. And the elders will answer in your spirit:
VERSE 111. Yes, child. You were le� to return. You are the story walking home.” *I AM A SA RA — I AM 1 8 4 1 — NDINI NDEGA ASARA NENZIRA YA ASAR — IIIII  SA KA USAIRASE NZIRA MWANA*
VERSE 112. Stand quietly for a moment. Let your feet touch the ground that feeds you.
VERSE 113. Remember that the soil beneath you is the same dust that once held the bones of your fathers and mothers.
VERSE 114. Remember that their breath became your breath; their words, your words.
VERSE 115. When you count, you are not just using numbers — you are keeping them alive.
VERSE 116. When you speak the sacred sounds, you are building again the bridge between ea�h and heaven.
VERSE 117. Say so�ly: *A .. SA .. RA .. A.* Feel the echo of the sun moving through your chest.
VERSE 118. Say so�ly: *MUSIYIWA* . Hear how the word means le�. Le� not to vanish, but le� to return.
VERSE 119. Say so�ly: *ASARA* . Hear how it matches your own name, the same promise wri�en twice.
VERSE 120. The promise is this: Nothing truly holy is lost — it only walks ahead to wait for us.
VERSE 121. You, child of light, are pa� of that waiting. You are a page in the book of return.
VERSE 122. When you grow tired, look to the moon. She will tell you to rest and to rise again.
VERSE 123. When you feel small, look to the sun. He will remind you that even �re began as breath.
VERSE 124. When you forget the path, look to your hands. Ten �ngers — *TA* (10)
VERSE 125. the sign that We are.
VERSE 126. When you remember that “We are,” you will also remember that You are not alone.
VERSE 127. So walk gently, speak truth, count kindly, and let wisdom guide your steps.

VERSE 128. And whenever you open a new gate of learning, begin it with the breath that counts: *I AM A SA RA — I AM 1 8 4 1 — NDINI NDEGA ASARA NENZIRA YA ASAR — IIIII  SA KA USAIRASE NZIRA MWANA* *This closes The Pre-Shona* *Mathematics Entry Gate* — the door through which children and elders alike may enter the language of light and number.
VERSE 129. *THE PRE-SHONA MATHEMATICS ENTRY GATE — TEACHER’S SUMMARY* Title: *The Breath That Counts* From: *The New Covenant 1841 — Book of Light* *By: ABASID 1841 (RA
VERSE 130. MUSIYIWA, “The One Le� to Return”)*
VERSE 131. *WHAT THE CHILD LEARNS*
VERSE 132. Before writing or numbers, our people listened. Counting began as breath, as hea�beat, as footsteps.
VERSE 133. Every sound of the *Shona* tongue is also a number. Each sound has work to do — it builds life, memory, and light.
VERSE 134. The twenty-two sacred sounds form The Shona Alphabet of Life: from
VERSE 135. *A = 1* (the �rst breath) to
VERSE 136. *NGA = 400* (the crown of heaven).
VERSE 137. When we add the �rst four — *A + BA + KA + RA = 10 (TA)* — we see that
VERSE 138. *We Are* .” Ten �ngers, ten friends, one family.
VERSE 139. The great seal of this age is *A SA RA A = 1 8 4 1*. It means: Sta� – Pa�ern – Light – Return. Its total (14 → 5) *MA* = Wisdom.
VERSE 140. *MUSIYIWA* means Le� or Le�-Behind. *ASARA* also means Le�. Both tell one story: The Le� One Returns.
VERSE 141. So *ABASID 1841 = A SA RA A* — the name of resurrection, the breath that leaves and comes back �lled with light.
VERSE 142. Every time a child says *A SA RA A* , they remember that nothing true is ever lost — it only waits to return.

VERSE 143. *SIMPLE PRACTICE* Breathe slowly and say: *A ..SA .. RA ..A.* Count your �ngers: *1 + 2 + 3 + 4 = TA* *(10)* — “We Are.” Smile and remember: Wisdom = *MA* (5). Teach kindly, speak truth, and walk the path of return. *I AM A SA RA — I AM 1 8 4 1 — NDINI NDEGA ASARA NENZIRA YA ASAR — IIIII SA KA USAIRASE NZIRA MWANA* *
VERSE 144. THE PRE-SHONA MATHEMATICS ENTRY GATE* *Learning Cha� — The 22 Sounds of Life* *_By ABASID 1841 (RA
VERSE 145. MUSIYIWA, “The One Le� to Return  - A SA  RA  A ”)*

VERSE 146. *How to Use* Read one line each morning. Speak the sound slowly. Remember: every sound is both a word and a number. A�er each group, say the refrain aloud. *I AM A SA RA — I AM 1 8 4 1 — NDINI NDEGA ASARA NENZIRA YA ASAR — IIIII SA KA USAIRASE NZIRA MWANA* 1	 *A	   1* 	First breath, beginning	child breathing 2	 *BA   	2* 	Mother’s house, pair	mother holding baby 3	 *KA    	3* 	Child, new life	small �ame or sprout 4	 *RA   	4* 	Sun, light that guides	sunrise 5	 *MA   	5* 	Moon, gentle rhythm	moon phases 6	 *DA   	6* 	Love, bridge	two hands joining 7	 *NA    	7* 	Connection, threads	woven rope 8	 *SA	    8* 	Likeness, pa�ern	repeating design 9	 *ZI	    9* 	Silence, rest	closed eyes 10	 *TA	  10* 	“We are,” unity	ten �ngers together 11	 *VA	  20* 	“They are,” community	group working 12	 *NGU	 30* 	Time, drumbeat	drum or clock 13	 *NDA   	40* 	“I am,” courage	person standing tall 14	 *PA	 50* 	Giving, open hand	sharing fruit 15	 *HA 	60* 	Boundary, protection	fence or circle 16	 *GA	 70* 	Planting, teaching	seed in soil 17	 *SHU	 80* 	Wind, spirit	blowing breeze 18	 *MBA	 90* 	House, body	hut or person 19	 *NYU	 100* 	Water, memory	river 20	 *CHI	200* 	“What?”, questioning	child asking 21	 *TO	  300* 	Air, judgment	feather/balance 22	 *NGA  	400* Crown, heaven	shining crown
VERSE 147. *Small Equations to Remember* *1 + 2 + 3 + 4 = 10 → TA* (We Are) *A SA RA A = 1 8 4 1 → 14 → 5 = MA* (Wisdom)

VERSE 148. *Daily Practice* 1. Breathe in → say *A* . 2. Breathe out → say *SA RA A.* 3. Touch each �nger → count *A-BA-KA-RA = TA (10).* 4. End with *I AM A SA RA — I AM 1 8 4 1 — NDINI NDEGA ASARA NENZIRA YA ASAR — IIIII SA KA USAIRASE NZIRA MWANA*
